steps.title: Identify the clue: Australia restricts which member nation
steps.content: The OECD has 38 member countries. The sentence specifies which member had a price of 113 USD. Since “Australia” must identify which of the 38 member nations is meant, it's the restricting element—essential for understanding which country we're discussing.

steps.title: Apply the rule for restrictive apposition
steps.content: Restrictive appositives—where the second noun is essential for identification—require no punctuation. Option A places “Australia” directly after “nation”, applying this rule to identify which of the 38 member nations is meant. Options B and D add punctuation suggesting non-restrictive apposition, while C hyphens the words as a compound. That matches choice A — A is correct.
